title: PQ1
description:
Practice Quiz PQ1 covers Lectures 1 and 2. It is based on assignments A1 and A2 and the examples covered in Lecture 1 and 2 supplements.

 

PQ1 is a timed quiz, meaning that once you open it you'll have to complete it within the allotted time. You have 10 attempts.

 

Please note: PQ1 is not graded.
descriptionText:
Practice Quiz PQ1 covers Lectures 1 and 2. It is based on assignments A1 and A2 and the examples covered in Lecture 1 and 2 supplements.
 
PQ1 is a timed quiz, meaning that once you open it you'll have to complete it within the allotted time. You have 10 attempts.
 
Please note: PQ1 is not graded.

questions.text:
The price of a share of Target stock is currently trading at $46.85. You want to buy 110 shares of the stock on margin. Your broker requires an initial margin of 60% and has a maintenance margin of 30%. Your broker charges an effective annual interest rate of 8% on margin loans.
 
Question 1: You plan to borrow as much as you can from your broker to finance your margin position. What is the most you can borrow from your broker?
Answer: 

Type one of the following choices: A, B, C, D or E:
A = $5153.5
B = $3944.2
C = $3092.1
D = $2101.4
E = $2061.4
 
Question 2: If the share price rises to $54.96 after 3 months, what is the equity in your account?
Answer: 

Type one of the following choices: A, B, C, D or E:
A = $5153.5
B = $3944.2
C = $3092.1
D = $2101.4
E = $2061.4
 
Question 3: If the share price rises to $54.96 after 3 months, what is the percentage margin (or percentage equity) in your account?
Answer: 

Type one of the following choices: A, B, C, D or E:
A = 164.73%
B = 107.54%
C = 65.24%
D = 27.56%
E = 14.72%
 
Question 4: What is the effective annual return over the 3 month period?
Answer: 

Type one of the following choices: A, B, C, D or E:
A = 164.73%
B = 107.54%
C = 65.24%
D = 27.56%
E = 14.72%
 
Question 5: What is the minimum value the stock price can take after 3 months before you are issued a margin call?
Answer: 

Type one of the following choices: A, B, C, D or E:
A = $31.45
B = $27.29
C = $26.77
D = $23.96
E = $21.4

questions.text:
The Dec 31 2028 US Treasury bond has a face value of $1000 and an annual coupon rate of 6% paid semi-annually. The bond trades currently at 98.7% of the face value (i.e., $987). You plan to buy 70 bonds on margin. Your broker requires an initial margin of 55% and has a maintenance margin of 27%. Your broker charges an effective annual interest rate of 4.6% on margin loans.
 
Question 1: You plan to borrow as much as you can from your broker to finance your margin position. What is the most you can borrow from your broker?
Answer: 

Type one of the following choices: A, B, C, D or E:
A = $69,090
B = $37,999.5
C = $36,221.4
D = $31,678.6
E = $31,090.5
 
Question 2: If the bond price drops to 94% of the face value after 5 months, and the bond makes exactly one semi-annual coupon payment during this period, what is the equity in your account?
Answer: 

Type one of the following choices: A, B, C, D or E:
A = $69,090
B = $37,999.5
C = $36,221.4
D = $31,678.6
E = $31,090.5
 
Question 3: If the bond price drops to 94% of the face value after 5 months, and the bond makes exactly one semi-annual coupon payment during this period, what is the percentage margin (or percentage equity) in your account?
Answer: 

Type one of the following choices: A, B, C, D or E:
A = -10.86%
B = -4.68%
C = 51.86%
D = 53.35%
E = 55.05%
 
Question 4: What is the effective annual return over the 5 month period?
Answer: 

Type one of the following choices: A, B, C, D or E:
A = -10.86%
B = -4.68%
C = 51.86%
D = 53.35%
E = 55.05%
 
Question 5: What is the minimum value the stock price can take after 5 months before you are issued a margin call?
Answer: 

Type one of the following choices: A, B, C, D or E:
A = $661.03
B = $619.93
C = $608.42
D = $578.84
E = $567.33

questions.text:
The price of a share of Intel stock is currently trading at $24.66. You want to short sale 370 shares of the stock. Your broker requires an initial margin of 50% and has a maintenance margin of 50%. 
 
Question 1: How much equity do you need in your account to be able to open the short position?
Answer: 

Type one of the following choices: A, B, C, D or E:
A = $13,686.3
B = $9,124.2
C = $5,009.8
D = $4,562.1
E = $3,985.3
 
Question 2: If the share price were to drop to $21.84 after 6 months and the stock paid one dividend of $1.61 per share during this period, what should be the equity in your account?
Answer: 

Type one of the following choices: A, B, C, D or E:
A = $13,686.3
B = $9,124.2
C = $5,009.8
D = $4,562.1
E = $3,985.3
 
Question 3: If the share price were to drop to $21.84 after 6 months and the stock paid one dividend of $1.61 per share during this period, what should be the percentage margin (or percentage equity) in your account?
Answer: 

Type one of the following choices: A, B, C, D or E:
A = 9.81%
B = 20.59%
C = 41.24%
D = 62%
E = 69.37%
 
Question 4: What is the effective annual return over the 6 month period?
Answer: 

Type one of the following choices: A, B, C, D or E:
A = 9.81%
B = 20.59%
C = 41.24%
D = 62%
E = 69.37%
 
Question 5: What is the maximum value the stock price can take after 6 months before you are issued a margin call?
Answer: 

Type one of the following choices: A, B, C, D or E:
A = $25.73
B = $24.66
C = $23.59
D = $18.92
E = $14.56

questions.text:
On Aug 1st 2025, a share of Tesla stock was trading at $460. You believed the stock was overvalued and decided to short sale 100 shares. Your broker requires an initial margin of 60% and has a maintenance margin of 60%. 
 
Question 1: How much equity did you need in your account to be able to open the short position on Aug 1st?
Answer: 

Type one of the following choices: A, B, C, D or E:
A = $73,600
B = $46,000
C = $33,600
D = $27,600
E = $23,600
 
Question 2: On Aug 15th, 2025, Tesla share price dropped to $400 per share. What was the equity in your account on Aug 15th?
Answer: 

Type one of the following choices: A, B, C, D or E:
A = $73,600
B = $46,000
C = $33,600
D = $27,600
E = $23,600
 
Question 3: On Aug 25th, 2025, Tesla announced that it plans to pay an extraordinary dividend of $20 per share in 6 month. The announcement led to an increase in Tesla share price to $500 per share. What was the equity in your account on Aug 25th?
Answer: 

Type one of the following choices: A, B, C, D or E:
A = $73,600
B = $46,000
C = $33,600
D = $27,600
E = $23,600
 
Question 4: Did you get a margin call on Aug 25th?
Answer: 

Type one of the following choices: A, B or C:
A = Yes
B = No
C = Lost my phone, so I have no idea ;)

questions.text:
A mutual fund consists of 100,000 shares of Stock A, 200,000 shares of Stock B, and 300,000 shares of stock C. The fund also has $2 million in cash, and $450,000 in liabilities. The fund has 2 million shares outstanding.
 
Question 1: Suppose that on Sep 1st 2025 the closing prices of the shares of Stocks A, B and C are respectively, $520, $410, and $350. What is the fund's NAV per share on Sep 1st?
Answer: 

Type one of the following choices: A, B, C, and D
A = $123.775
B = $120.275
C = $119.5
D = $117.75
E = $112.25
 
Question 2: Suppose that on Sep 5th 2025 the closing prices of the shares of Stocks A, B and C are respectively, $550, $400, and $370. Suppose a large fund shareholder wants to redeem 200,000 of the fund shares. What is the fund's NAV after the shareholder redeemed its fund shares?  
Answer: 

Type one of the following choices: A, B, C, and D
A = $123.775
B = $120.275
C = $119.5
D = $117.75
E = $112.25
 
Question 3: Suppose that the fund had operating expenses of 0.9%, b-12 fees of 0.5%, and a back-end load of 3%, if the fund is sold within one year, and 0%, if the fund is sold after one year. If the fund generates an average net return of 12.5% per year before fees, how much money would an investor make if it invested $100,000 in the fund for 3 years?
Answer: 

Type one of the following choices: A, B, C, and D
A = $130,229.34
B = $132,451.62
C = $133,019.07
D = $137,133.06
E = $138,992.89
